real DBOS naked @DBOS.step.note: FAILURE EXAMPLE, not the strong baseline

real DBOS + idempotent effect (recommend.note: STRONG baseline -- matches AgentTx on simple effects (1/1)

evidence_for_AgentTx.effect_XO_wellbehaved: phase3 (300 crashes/class), phase7 (400 turns x K procs, 0 double)
evidence_for_AgentTx.mid_effect_nonatomic: phase6 tau2 mid-crash: AgentTx 15/15 vs naive 10
evidence_for_AgentTx.KV_plane: view* = byte-exact fail-closed CAS (phase2) + durable cross-process reload (phase8 kv_durable); inject-into-fresh-engine-attention is TARGET

headline: On simple-effect exactly-once, a properly-IDEMPOTENT DBOS matches AgentTx (both 1/1) -- naked DBOS (2 files) is only the failure example. AgentTx's differentiation is the columns NO baseline fills together: DISTRIBUTED concurrent recovery (fencing), MID-EFFECT crashes on NON-ATOMIC effects, a DURABLE output plane, and a single cross-plane turn-prefix contract over all of them.
note: Temporal/Atomix/Cordon/Concordia/Crab rows are from their papers (not re-run here); see docs/RELATED_BASELINES.md. DBOS naked/idempotent + LangGraph + AgentTx are measured.
